titles.text: 出たな!! TwinBee ツインビー

descriptions.text:
「ツインビー」シリーズの「キュート・エム・アップ」スタイルのトップダウンシューティングゲームです。

「デタナ!!」では、ツインビーとウィンビーのパイロットであるパステルとライトが初めて登場し、悪の力から世界を救うためにメローラ姫を救う旅を描いています。それでも、6つの縦スクロールアクションステージで、シュールでかわいい敵を撃ちまくることがメインです。

パワーアップやアップグレードは、敵が残すカラーベルを撃つことで得られ、希望の色になるまで待つことができます。多くの組み合わせが可能です。基本武器に新しいチャージショット機能が追加され、シングルプレイと2人協力プレイの両方が楽しめます。
The PC-Engine port of Bells & Whistles originally released only in Japan before coming to the West through the Wii Virtual Console service, under its original Japanese title.

Detana!! TwinBee, released in Europe as Bells & Whistles, is a 1991 vertically scrolling shooter video game developed and published by Konami for arcades. It is the fifth entry in the TwinBee series and the second to be released for arcades following the original TwinBee (previous sequels were released directly to home consoles). Set several years after the events of TwinBee, players assume the role of Light and Pastel (marking their debut appearance) taking control of TwinBee and WinBee to defeat invading forces of the evil alien Iva and save planet Meru after receiving an SOS message sent by Princess Melora.
Detana!! TwinBee marked the debut of Japanese animator Shūjirō Hamakawa (credited under the pen name Shuzilow.Ha) as primary character designer for subsequent installments of the TwinBee series. The game proved popular among Japanese arcade players, earning several awards from Gamest magazine, while its ports to other platforms were also met with positive response from critics. A direct follow-up, Pop'n TwinBee, was released for the Super Nintendo Entertainment System in 1993.
